source: GPAI Provider Terms of Use - Curated Summaries
note: Summarized restrictions relevant to EU AI Act Article 25(1)(c) analysis. Check original ToS for latest versions. These summaries are for classification purposes only and do not constitute legal advice.

prohibited_uses_relevant_to_annex_iii: Automated decision-making in legal, employment, credit, or insurance contexts without human oversight
Surveillance or facial recognition without consent
Law enforcement profiling based on protected characteristics
Political campaigning or lobbying without disclosure
Generating content to manipulate elections or democratic processes
high_risk_restrictions: Must not use for automated legal, financial, or employment decisions without appropriate human review
Must not use to generate content designed to mislead voters
Must not use for real-time biometric identification in public spaces
openai.intended_purpose_statement: General-purpose conversational AI assistant and API for text generation, analysis, and creative tasks
openai.eu_ai_act_relevance: OpenAI's terms restrict but do not prevent high-risk uses. A deployer using GPT-4 for resume screening likely violates both ToS AND triggers Article 25(1)(c).

prohibited_uses_relevant_to_annex_iii: Autonomous decision-making in high-stakes domains (criminal justice, immigration, credit) without human oversight
Surveillance or tracking individuals without consent
Generating content to manipulate elections
Weapons development or military applications causing harm
high_risk_restrictions: Requires meaningful human oversight for consequential decisions
Must not be sole decision-maker in credit, employment, housing, insurance, or legal contexts
Must not be used for mass surveillance
anthropic.intended_purpose_statement: AI assistant for conversation, analysis, coding, and creative tasks
anthropic.eu_ai_act_relevance: Anthropic's AUP explicitly flags high-stakes autonomous decision-making. Using Claude as the sole screener for job applications would violate AUP and trigger Article 25(1)(c).

prohibited_uses_relevant_to_annex_iii: Making automated decisions about individuals in employment, credit, housing, insurance, education, or legal contexts
Surveillance in violation of applicable law
Generating content to interfere with democratic processes
Real-time biometric identification for law enforcement
high_risk_restrictions: Must not use for fully automated consequential decisions about individuals
Requires disclosure when users interact with AI
Must not use for social scoring or ranking individuals
google.intended_purpose_statement: General-purpose multimodal AI model for text, image, audio, and video understanding and generation
google.eu_ai_act_relevance: Google's terms prohibit automated consequential decisions. Using Gemini for credit scoring would violate ToS and trigger Article 25(1)(c).

prohibited_uses_relevant_to_annex_iii: Automated decision-making that produces legal effects or similarly significant effects on individuals
Real-time facial recognition in public spaces
Political manipulation
Predictive policing based on profiling
high_risk_restrictions: Must implement human review for high-impact decisions
Must comply with applicable laws including non-discrimination requirements
Azure OpenAI requires additional approval for sensitive use cases (limited access features)
Must not use for fully autonomous decisions in employment, credit, or insurance
microsoft.intended_purpose_statement: AI-powered productivity and development tools, cloud-based AI services
microsoft.eu_ai_act_relevance: Microsoft requires additional approval for sensitive uses via Azure OpenAI. Using Copilot for employee performance evaluation without approval likely violates ToS and triggers Article 25(1)(c).

prohibited_uses_relevant_to_annex_iii: Automated decision-making in domains that affect an individual's rights (criminal justice, law enforcement, immigration, credit, employment, housing, insurance, education, social scoring)
Surveillance and biometric identification without consent
Generating content for election interference or manipulation of democratic processes
high_risk_restrictions: Must not use for fully automated decisions in high-stakes domains without meaningful human oversight
Must not use for real-time or post-biometric identification in public spaces for law enforcement
Must not use for social scoring or ranking of individuals
Users must comply with Acceptable Use Policy even for self-hosted deployments
meta.intended_purpose_statement: Open-weight large language models for research and commercial use in text generation, reasoning, coding, and conversation
meta.eu_ai_act_relevance: Meta's Llama models are open-weight, meaning deployers often fine-tune them — which can trigger Article 25(1)(b) (substantial modification) in addition to Article 25(1)(c) (modified intended purpose). Self-hosting Llama for credit scoring creates dual triggers.
meta.open_weight_note: Because Llama models are open-weight, deployers frequently fine-tune or adapt them. This creates a higher likelihood of triggering Article 25(1)(b) in addition to 25(1)(c). Organizations self-hosting Llama should be especially vigilant.

prohibited_uses_relevant_to_annex_iii: Automated decision-making that produces legal or similarly significant effects on individuals
Biometric identification or categorisation in violation of applicable law
Generating content to manipulate elections or democratic processes
Surveillance in violation of applicable law
high_risk_restrictions: Must not use for fully autonomous consequential decisions about natural persons
Must comply with all applicable EU regulations including the AI Act
Must implement appropriate human oversight for high-stakes use cases
mistral.intended_purpose_statement: General-purpose and specialized language models for text generation, coding, reasoning, and multimodal tasks
mistral.eu_ai_act_relevance: Mistral, as an EU-based provider, is directly subject to the AI Act. However, deployers using Mistral models for Annex III purposes still trigger Article 25(1)(c). Some Mistral models are open-weight, creating similar dual-trigger risks as Meta Llama.
mistral.open_weight_note: Some Mistral models (Mixtral, Mistral Nemo) are open-weight. Same dual-trigger risk as Meta Llama for fine-tuned deployments.
